data.title: Palo Alto Networks receives fast-track inclusion on Israel's key indices: report
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (PANW) has received fast-track inclusion to appear on Israel's TA-35 and TA-125 indices, according to the Calcalist.

The fast track allows the cybersecurity company to be added on August 6, and its maximum weight on the indices will be capped at 5%, the report said. 

Palo Alto Networks began trading on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange in February 2026, after completing its acquisition of the Israel-based CyberArk, an identity security firm. Palo Alto was founded by Nir Zuk and Rajiv Batra in 2005.

data.title: Lucid reports Q2 deliveries, taps auto exec for top finance position
data.description:
Lucid manufactured 4,774 vehicles in the second quarter and delivered 3,953, an increase of 23% and 19%, respectively, from a year ago, but less than Wall Street expected.

Along with second quarter metrics, the company also announced organizational changes and streamlined its corporate suite, with the number of direct reports to CEO Silvio Napoli cut in half.

As part of these changes, auto executive Alexander De Bock has been named Lucid’s (LCID) new chief financial officer, replacing Taoufiq Boussaid, with Raja Ramana Macha tapped as chief technology officer.

Other appointments include Billy Hayes as chief customer officer, Hugo Martinho as chief transformation officer, Kay Stepper as president of Lucid Technologies and chief digital officer, and Christian Appel promoted to VP of Program Management.

These changes were announced last week and enable the company to reduce organizational complexity, align manufacturing capacity with market demand, and improve competitiveness.

Lucid (LCID) shares are down more than 3% on Thursday.

data.title: The Russell 2000 outperforms the S&P 500 for second consecutive quarter in Q2
data.description:
For a second consecutive quarter, the Russell 2000 (IWM) has outperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) in Q2, the strongest performance since the early pandemic quarters.

The Russell 2000 (IWM) is up almost 22% year-to-date and up about 3.8% from a month ago. The S&P 500 (SP500), on the other hand, has risen only 9.3% year-to-date, while being down 0.9% from a month ago.

Among the top-performing Russell 2000 (IWM) stocks last quarter were Ichor Holdings (ICHR), Credo Technology (CRDO), and Par Pacific Holdings (PARR).

Moreover, 43 of the highest-performing stocks within the index have moved into the large-cap Russell 1000 index (IWB) in late June.

data.title: Hassett seems to confirm Trump administration is talking to AI firms about a possible stake
data.description:
National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett seemed to confirm that OpenAI (OPENAI) has proposed giving the U.S. government a 5% stake in the artificial intelligence company.

“We’re talking to all the big AI companies and seeing what they think about what they could do to share their upside with the American people,” Hassett said in a Thursday interview with CNBC. Hassett did not give further details on what the discussions entailed. 

However, Hassett said it was an “open question” of whether the U.S. federal government would take a stake in companies, similar to what  it did with Intel (INTC). He described the Intel stake as being driven by “unique” circumstances.

The Financial Times reported that OpenAI has discussed giving a 5% stake to the U.S. federal government as the $852B AI start-up seeks to clear political obstacles. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has argued that giving the public a financial stake in the company is the best way to share the upside of AI. 

Altman has been in active talks with the Trump administration about the issue of public ownership, including President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, according to multiple people familiar with the matter.

The proposed arrangement would involve other U.S. AI companies handing over a similar stake, although it is not clear if the other labs would be willing to do so, the report said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Agnico Eagle suspends operations at Barnat pit after rock movement
data.description:
Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) down 1.4% in early trading Thursday after saying it has temporarily suspended mining operations at the Barnat open pit at its Canadian Malartic complex in Quebec after a rock mass movement occurred along the mine's north wall.

The company said the area had previously been identified as having weaker geological structures and was under enhanced geotechnical monitoring and that technical teams are conducting a detailed assessment to determine next steps.

During the suspension of in-pit mining operations, the Canadian Malartic processing plant will be supplied with low-grade ore from existing stockpiles in place of planned Barnat ore feed, the company said.

Agnico Eagle (AEM) said it expects the incident to reduce production at Canadian Malartic in this year's H2 by 60K-80K oz of gold, which could push full-year output toward the lower end of its previously disclosed guidance range of 3.3M-3.5M oz.

The Barnat open pit was expected to be mined out by early 2029, and Agnico Eagle (AEM) said the incident could reduce production by as much as 150K oz/year of gold in both 2027 and 2028, pending the outcome of its geotechnical assessment.

Q2 gold production at Canadian Malartic was not affected, which the company expects at ~845K oz, slightly ahead of plan.

data.title: Least shorted U.S. small-cap stocks
data.description:
Investors remain largely optimistic on several U.S. small-cap stocks, with the latest short-interest data showing minimal bearish positioning across a handful of companies with market capitalizations below $2B.

Low short interest generally reflects relatively positive market sentiment, although it may also indicate lower trading activity or fewer catalysts drawing bearish bets.

The least shorted U.S. stocks with market capitalizations below $2B are:

  • Legacy Education (LGCY): 0.50% short interest
  • AmeriServ Financial (ASRV): 0.50%
  • PodcastOne (PODC): 0.50%
  • DHI Group (DHX): 0.50%
  • Cardio Diagnostics Holdings (CDIO): 0.50%
  • Century Casinos (CNTY): 0.50%
  • Onconetix (ONCO): 0.50%
  • Acorn Energy (ACFN): 0.51%
  • Ramaco Resources (METC): 0.51%
  • Agora (API): 0.51%

Small-cap ETFs offering exposure to this segment include iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM), Vanguard Small-Cap ETF (VB), iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR), Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF (VTWO), Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) and SPDR Portfolio S&P 600 Small Cap ETF (SPSM).

data.title: Most shorted U.S. stocks under $2B
data.description:
Bearish sentiment remains elevated across several U.S. small-cap stocks, with restaurant operator Jack in the Box (JACK) topping the list of the most shorted companies with market capitalizations below $2B. The stock had short interest of 35.21%, according to the latest data.

Healthcare, artificial intelligence, electric vehicle charging, and space exploration companies also featured prominently, highlighting broad investor skepticism across multiple sectors.

The most shorted U.S. stocks with market capitalizations below $2B are:

  • Jack in the Box (JACK): 35.21%
  • Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX): 35.07%
  • Fabric AI (FABC): 34.91%
  • Virgin Galactic (SPCE): 34.41%
  • LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ): 33.10%
  • EVgo (EVGO): 32.85%
  • Groupon (GRPN): 32.78%
  • Dave & Buster's (PLAY): 30.76%
  • indie Semiconductor (INDI): 30.36%
  • C3.ai (AI): 29.77%

Short interest measures the percentage of a company's tradable shares that have been sold short by investors betting the stock price will decline. While elevated short interest often signals bearish sentiment, it can also increase the likelihood of a short squeeze if positive news or stronger-than-expected results force short sellers to buy back shares.

Small-cap ETFs include the iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM), Vanguard Small-Cap ETF (VB), iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR), Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF (VTWO), Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA), and SPDR Portfolio S&P 600 Small Cap ETF (SPSM).

data.title: Hagerty signs $43M deal to acquire UK's Bennetts
data.description:
• Hagerty (NYSE:HGTY) said Thursday it agreed to acquire U.K. specialty motorcycle insurance broker Bennetts from Lucida Group for £34M ($43M).
  • The deal is expected to close in Q3 2026, subject to regulatory approval, and be immediately accretive.
  • The acquisition is expected to triple Hagerty's U.K. revenue to about £25M while adding Bennetts' 15% share of the U.K. motorcycle insurance market.
  • Shares +1.17%.

data.title: Ford Q2 sales down 10.3% to 549,200
data.description:
• Ford (F) sold 10.3% fewer vehicles in the second quarter, with electric vehicle sales cut nearly in half and hybrid sales down 20% from the same quarter last year.
  • According to recent data released by the automaker, Ford (F) sold 549,200 vehicles in Q2, notching lower sales for SUVs and trucks, while car sales were up 3.4%.
  • Year-to-date, total vehicle sales are down 9.6% from the same period last year, with EV sales down 57.4%, hybrids down 20%, and ICE vehicle sales down 6.4%.
  • For the month of June, Ford (F) increased its market share by 0.2 percentage points to 12.3%.
  • Ford (F) shares opened higher with a gain of 1.5%.

data.title: Canada's manufacturing growth accelerates to 53.0 in June
data.description: Manufacturing PMI in Canada increased to 53 points in June from 52.90 points in May of 2026.
